intro: Pentaspadon curtisii, also known as the Malaysian liverwort, is a type of bryophyte, a non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Podocarpaceae. Traditionally, Pentaspadon curtisii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including liver and kidney disorders, due to its purported detoxifying and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentaspadon curtisii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pentaspadon curtisii is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used to make a topical paste for treating skin conditions and wounds.
